# PointNetVLAD: Deep Point Cloud Based Retrieval for Large-Scale Place Recognition

- **Work ID:** `LPR-094`
- **Reviewed version:** `doi:10.1109/cvpr.2018.00470`
- **Year / venue / type:** 2018 / CVPR / `conference`
- **Curation tier:** `evidence_reviewed`
- **Relevance:** `core_lpr`
- **Representative tier:** `ordinary`
- **Primary branch:** [Point-based learning](../landscape.md#branch-global-retrieval-learned-point)

## Accepted structured facts

- **datasets:** Oxford RobotCar, University Sector (U.S.), Residential Area (R.A.), Business District (B.D.) — `source_fact`; manual, p. 5–6, § 5.1 Benchmark Datasets, Table 1;Fig. 4
- **input_unit:** single submap with ground removed, downsampled to 4096 points, centered and scaled to \[-1,1\] — `source_fact`; manual, p. 5, § 5.1 Benchmark Datasets - Submap preprocessing, Fig. 3
- **invariances:** point-order permutation, illumination/weather/season robustness — `source_fact`; manual, p. 5–8, § 4.3 Permutation Invariance;5.2 Image comparisons under changing scene conditions, Fig. 6(c)
- **representation:** PointNet local features, NetVLAD aggregation, 256-D L2-normalized global descriptor, lazy quadruplet loss — `source_fact`; manual, p. 3–4, § 4. Our PointNetVLAD;4.1 Network Architecture;4.2 Metric Learning, Fig. 2;Eq. 1-3
- **uses_learning:** yes — `source_fact`; manual, p. 1–4, § Abstract;4.2 Metric Learning, Eq. 2-3
- **uses_sequence:** no — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 3, § 3. Problem Definition
